refined_fr.refined_fr: CR-NGHE-003 Refined: The NGHE solution shall enable operators and tele-operators to achieve a measured energy efficiency of energy_per_ton ≤ E_target (customer-defined threshold) over the agreed evaluation period, through integrated monitoring, control optimization, and real-time feedback mechanisms.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is outcome-focused but lacks explicit linkage to the mechanisms (monitoring, control, feedback) that enable efficiency achievement. Refinement clarifies that energy efficiency is not a passive property but an active capability requiring measurement, visibility, and operator decision support. This aligns with the high safety criticality constraint by ensuring operators have visibility into energy consumption patterns.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure energy_per_ton = (total_energy_consumed_kWh / total_mass_moved_tons) across a defined evaluation period (e.g., 40-hour work week). Verify that measured value ≤ E_target. Collect telemetry from Vehicle power systems and cross-validate with site energy meters. Require operator sign-off on measurement methodology and baseline conditions.
sub_frs: CR-NGHE-003.1: The NGHE solution shall continuously measure and log energy consumption (kWh) at the Vehicle level with ±5% accuracy.
CR-NGHE-003.2: The NGHE solution shall continuously measure and log mass moved (tons) at the Vehicle level with ±3% accuracy.
CR-NGHE-003.3: The NGHE solution shall compute and display energy_per_ton KPI in real-time to the Operator and Tele-operator via Telemetry interface.
CR-NGHE-003.4: The NGHE solution shall provide predictive or advisory feedback to Operator/Tele-operator on actions that reduce energy_per_ton without compromising safety or task completion.
CR-NGHE-003.5: The NGHE solution shall enforce operational limits (e.g., speed governors, load restrictions) if energy_per_ton trajectory indicates target miss, with SafetyAlert notification.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Passive energy monitoring only (measure and log, no active control or feedback)
DP-B: Active energy monitoring + real-time KPI display + operator advisory feedback (no enforcement)
DP-C: Active energy monitoring + real-time KPI display + operator advisory feedback + automated enforcement limits with safety interlocks
DP-D: Predictive energy modeling with machine learning to forecast energy_per_ton and recommend task sequencing/routing changes
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-B: Active energy monitoring + real-time KPI display + operator advisory feedback (no enforcement). Rationale: Balances the need for visibility and decision support (required to achieve efficiency target) with operational flexibility and safety (avoids over-constraining the operator). DP-C introduces enforcement complexity and potential safety conflicts; DP-D requires data maturity not yet established. DP-A is insufficient to enable target achievement.

potential_sources: Energy measurement (Vehicle power telemetry) is decoupled from advisory feedback logic (Safety Services or Teleoperation layer can consume Telemetry and generate advisory SafetyAlert).
Real-time KPI display depends on low-latency Telemetry interface; this is a known interface and does not create hidden coupling.
Operator advisory feedback is independent of task execution (Tasking interface); feedback is informational, not prescriptive.
coupling_check.notes: DP-B exhibits loose coupling: measurement is orthogonal to feedback generation. No circular dependencies detected. Assumption: Telemetry interface supports ≤500ms latency for real-time display; if latency exceeds 2s, advisory feedback loses credibility.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: Vehicle power systems can provide energy consumption data with ±5% accuracy. Risk: Legacy or third-party Vehicle subsystems may lack instrumentation; mitigation required in Vehicle DP.
Assumption: Operator and Tele-operator will act on advisory feedback. Risk: Behavioral compliance is not guaranteed; recommend training and incentive alignment with Site Manager.

Assumption: Energy_per_ton target (E_target) is stable and achievable within normal operational envelope. Risk: If E_target is set below physical capability, DP-B will fail; recommend baseline study before target lock.
Assumption: Telemetry and SafetyAlert interfaces are available and reliable. Risk: Network Link failures could blind operators to KPI; recommend fallback display on Vehicle HMI.

Risk: High safety criticality constraint may conflict with advisory-only approach if energy efficiency is achieved by unsafe shortcuts (e.g., excessive speed). Mitigation: Safety Services must validate advisory recommendations before transmission.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Clarify in DP-B that advisory feedback is generated only for actions that maintain or improve safety margin. Add explicit gate: 'Safety Services shall validate all advisory recommendations before display to Operator.' This ensures DP-B does not inadvertently enable unsafe efficiency gains.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution IM is the top-level system boundary that orchestrates energy monitoring, KPI computation, and advisory feedback across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services modules. It owns the energy efficiency KPI logic and the integration of telemetry streams into actionable feedback.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Inbound from Vehicle: energy consumption (kWh), mass moved (tons), operational state (idle/active/fault). Required for KPI computation. Frequency: ≥1 Hz for real-time display.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Inbound from Teleoperation or Site Manager: task definition, priority, constraints. Used to contextualize advisory feedback (e.g., 'current task is time-critical; energy optimization deferred').

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Outbound to Operator/Tele-operator: advisory notifications on energy efficiency status, recommended actions, and safety-validated recommendations. Also inbound from Safety Services to validate advisory content before transmission.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Bidirectional communication backbone for Telemetry, Tasking, and SafetyAlert. NGHE Solution depends on Network Link reliability for real-time KPI visibility.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the natural top-level owner of energy efficiency KPI logic. Energy monitoring and KPI computation are core to the mission. Diffusion risk is minimal: only the integration of Telemetry streams and advisory feedback generation are contained here; actual energy measurement remains in Vehicle, and safety validation remains in Safety Services.

Vehicle owns energy measurement hardware and low-level telemetry. However, KPI computation (energy_per_ton) and advisory feedback logic should not be embedded in Vehicle; this would create tight coupling and make feedback logic hard to update. Vehicle should provide raw telemetry only. Diffusion risk: if KPI logic is scattered across Vehicle firmware and NGHE Solution, maintenance and testing become fragmented.

Teleoperation could host advisory feedback display and operator interaction logic. However, KPI computation should remain in NGHE Solution to avoid duplication if both Operator and Tele-operator need the same KPI. Diffusion risk: if Teleoperation owns advisory feedback, local Operator (non-tele) loses visibility. Recommend Teleoperation consumes SafetyAlert from NGHE Solution rather than owning KPI logic.

Safety Services should validate advisory recommendations before they are transmitted to operators (gate function). Safety Services owns the safety criticality assessment. However, Safety Services should not own the energy KPI computation itself; that is a performance metric, not a safety function. Appropriate role: Safety Services validates and gates advisory content, then NGHE Solution transmits via SafetyAlert.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: NGHE Solution owns the energy efficiency KPI logic (measurement aggregation, energy_per_ton computation, advisory recommendation generation). Vehicle provides raw telemetry (energy, mass, state). Safety Services validates advisory recommendations for safety compliance. Teleoperation and Operator HMI consume SafetyAlert notifications. This distribution minimizes diffusion: KPI logic is centralized, measurement is localized to Vehicle, and safety gating is localized to Safety Services.
containment_risks: Risk: If Telemetry interface is unreliable or high-latency, real-time KPI display becomes stale, reducing operator trust in advisory feedback. Mitigation: Require Telemetry SLA (≤500ms latency, ≥99% availability). Implement fallback display on Vehicle HMI using local energy data.

Risk: If Safety Services validation of advisory recommendations is slow or blocking, operators may ignore SafetyAlert notifications. Mitigation: Require Safety Services to validate recommendations asynchronously and cache results; NGHE Solution should not block on Safety Services response.

Risk: If E_target is not achievable within the Vehicle's physical envelope, NGHE Solution will continuously generate 'target miss' alerts, creating alert fatigue. Mitigation: Require baseline energy study and E_target validation before deployment. Implement alert suppression if target miss is due to external factors (e.g., terrain, load type).

Risk: Diffusion of energy measurement logic: if Vehicle firmware computes energy_per_ton locally and NGHE Solution also computes it, discrepancies will confuse operators. Mitigation: Enforce single source of truth: Vehicle provides raw telemetry only; NGHE Solution computes KPI.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Explicitly define the boundary between Vehicle telemetry (raw data: energy, mass, state) and NGHE Solution KPI logic (computed: energy_per_ton, advisory recommendations). Add a contract: 'Vehicle shall provide energy consumption and mass moved as separate telemetry fields; NGHE Solution shall compute energy_per_ton and shall be the sole source of KPI truth.' This prevents diffusion and ensures consistency across Operator and Tele-operator displays.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-B (active monitoring + real-time KPI display + advisory feedback) directly addresses the refined FR (enable operators to achieve energy_per_ton ≤ E_target). The DP provides the three mechanisms required: visibility (KPI display), decision support (advisory feedback), and feedback loop (real-time updates). No logical gap detected. Assumption: Operator behavioral compliance is assumed; this is a known risk but does not invalidate the DP-FR mapping.

diffusion_risks: Diffusion Risk 1: Energy measurement logic scattered across Vehicle firmware and NGHE Solution. Mitigation: Enforce single source of truth (Vehicle provides raw telemetry; NGHE Solution computes KPI).
Diffusion Risk 2: Advisory feedback logic duplicated in Teleoperation and NGHE Solution. Mitigation: NGHE Solution owns advisory generation; Teleoperation consumes SafetyAlert.

Diffusion Risk 3: Safety validation of advisory recommendations could be bypassed if Safety Services is not in the critical path. Mitigation: Require SafetyAlert to be generated only after Safety Services validation gate.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution is the appropriate owner of KPI logic (containment_score 95). Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services have clear, non-overlapping roles: Vehicle measures, Safety Services validates, Teleoperation displays. No module is overloaded. Interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link) are all known and available. No new interfaces required.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible: energy_per_ton can be measured directly from Vehicle telemetry and compared against E_target. Acceptance criteria are quantitative and testable. Evaluation period is defined by customer. Operator sign-off on measurement methodology is a clear gate. No hidden verification dependencies.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Allocation is clear: NGHE Solution owns KPI computation and advisory feedback. Vehicle owns energy measurement. Safety Services owns validation. Teleoperation owns display. Each module has a single, well-defined responsibility. No ambiguity in ownership or interface contracts.

recommended_revisions: Revision 1: Add explicit contract to DP-B: 'Safety Services shall validate all advisory recommendations for safety compliance before NGHE Solution transmits via SafetyAlert. Validation shall not block real-time KPI display; validation results shall be cached and updated asynchronously.'

Revision 2: Add explicit contract to IM assignment: 'Vehicle shall provide energy consumption (kWh) and mass moved (tons) as separate, timestamped telemetry fields. NGHE Solution shall compute energy_per_ton = energy / mass and shall be the sole source of KPI truth. No other module shall compute or display energy_per_ton.'

Revision 3: Add fallback mechanism: 'If Network Link fails, Vehicle HMI shall display local energy_per_ton (computed from Vehicle-local data) with a 'Network Offline' indicator. NGHE Solution shall resume KPI computation and display when Network Link is restored.'

Revision 4: Add baseline study gate: 'Before E_target is locked, a baseline energy study shall be conducted over ≥40 hours of representative work. E_target shall be set to a value achievable within the Vehicle's physical envelope and normal operational constraints. If E_target is not achievable, the FR shall be revised or the Vehicle DP shall be revised.'

decision.justification: The refined FR CR-NGHE-003 is not atomic; it decomposes into five sub-FRs (measurement, KPI computation, real-time display, advisory feedback, and enforcement limits). DP-B and IM assignment (NGHE Solution) are sound and pass all gates, but the sub-FRs require further decomposition to allocate responsibility across Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services, and NGHE Solution. Specifically, sub-FRs 003.1 and 003.2 (energy and mass measurement) must be decomposed into Vehicle-level DPs and IMs. Sub-FRs 003.3, 003.4, and 003.5 must be decomposed into NGHE Solution and Safety Services DPs and IMs. Proceeding to the next level will clarify the detailed design and identify any hidden coupling or diffusion risks.
decision.justification_detail: The current level (CR-NGHE-003) is a system-level FR that spans multiple modules. Decomposition is necessary to ensure each module has a clear, atomic responsibility. The sub-FRs are well-defined and ready for DP selection and IM assignment at the next level.
next_level_sub_frs: CR-NGHE-003.1: The Vehicle shall continuously measure and log energy consumption (kWh) with ±5% accuracy and transmit via Telemetry interface.
CR-NGHE-003.2: The Vehicle shall continuously measure and log mass moved (tons) with ±3% accuracy and transmit via Telemetry interface.
CR-NGHE-003.3: The NGHE Solution shall compute energy_per_ton = energy / mass in real-time and display to Operator and Tele-operator via SafetyAlert interface.
CR-NGHE-003.4: The NGHE Solution shall generate advisory recommendations to reduce energy_per_ton, validated by Safety Services, and transmit via SafetyAlert interface.

CR-NGHE-003.5: The NGHE Solution shall enforce operational limits (speed governors, load restrictions) if energy_per_ton trajectory indicates target miss, with SafetyAlert notification and operator override capability.

assumptions: Assumption 1: Vehicle power systems can provide energy consumption data with ±5% accuracy. This is a critical assumption; if Vehicle lacks instrumentation, the entire FR cannot be satisfied.
Assumption 2: Operator and Tele-operator will act on advisory feedback. Behavioral compliance is not guaranteed; training and incentive alignment are required.
Assumption 3: E_target is stable, achievable, and has been validated via baseline study. If E_target is set below physical capability, the FR will fail.
Assumption 4: Telemetry and SafetyAlert interfaces are available, reliable, and have latency ≤500ms. If latency exceeds 2s, real-time KPI display loses credibility.
Assumption 5: Safety Services can validate advisory recommendations without blocking real-time KPI display. Asynchronous validation is required.
Assumption 6: Network Link is available and reliable. If Network Link fails, fallback to Vehicle-local KPI display is acceptable.
open_risks: Risk 1: Vehicle instrumentation gap. Mitigation: Conduct Vehicle capability assessment before DP lock. If energy measurement is not available, revise Vehicle DP to add instrumentation.
Risk 2: Operator behavioral compliance. Mitigation: Implement training program and incentive alignment with Site Manager. Monitor advisory feedback acceptance rate.
Risk 3: E_target not achievable. Mitigation: Conduct baseline energy study over ≥40 hours. Lock E_target only after validation. If target is not achievable, revise FR or Vehicle DP.
Risk 4: Telemetry latency exceeds 2s. Mitigation: Require Telemetry SLA (≤500ms latency). Implement fallback display on Vehicle HMI. Monitor latency in production.

Risk 5: Safety Services validation is slow or blocking. Mitigation: Require asynchronous validation. Cache validation results. Implement timeout and fallback (display advisory without validation if timeout occurs).
Risk 6: Network Link failure. Mitigation: Implement fallback display on Vehicle HMI. Require Network Link SLA (≥99% availability). Monitor Network Link health.
Risk 7: Diffusion of energy measurement logic. Mitigation: Enforce single source of truth (Vehicle provides raw telemetry; NGHE Solution computes KPI). Add code review gate to prevent diffusion.
evidence_needed: Evidence 1: Vehicle capability assessment confirming energy measurement accuracy ±5% and mass measurement accuracy ±3%.
Evidence 2: Baseline energy study (≥40 hours) establishing achievable energy_per_ton range and validating E_target.
Evidence 3: Telemetry interface specification confirming latency ≤500ms and availability ≥99%.
Evidence 4: Safety Services validation logic specification and performance characterization (validation latency, timeout behavior).
Evidence 5: Operator training plan and incentive alignment strategy with Site Manager.
Evidence 6: Network Link SLA and monitoring plan.
Evidence 7: Code review checklist to prevent diffusion of energy measurement logic across Vehicle and NGHE Solution.
Evidence 8: Fallback display specification for Vehicle HMI (energy_per_ton display when Network Link is offline).
